channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world

item.title: Greek official accuses EU of policy failure on migration as war and climate change fuel displacement
item.description: A Greek official has criticized the outgoing European Commission for failing to forge a common policy to deport migrants and warned that war and climate change were increasing global displacement.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Conkers controversy: World tournament investigates claims of cheating with steel chestnut
item.description: The World Conker Championships says it's investigating allegations of cheating after this year’s men’s winner was found to have a steel chestnut in his pocket.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Deadly Israel shooting ruled a terror attack
item.description: A police officer was killed and four others were injured when a terrorist opened fire near a highway in Ashdod, Israel, on Tuesday.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Israel reveals Hezbollah special forces terrorist 'bunker' located under home with weapons, motorcycles
item.description: Israel's military has uncovered a bunker it says Hezbollah's special forces unit was using to plan a cross-border attack on Israeli citizens.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Springfield rhetoric reverberates in Florida's Haitian community
item.description: Republican attacks on Haitian migrants in Ohio are reverberating in Florida, where Democrats are hoping to mobilize the country's largest Haitian American electorate to close the gap in their uphill battle for statewide races.
item.dc:creator: Joanne Haner, The Hill

item.title: Taliban-run media stops showing images of living beings in some Afghan provinces
item.description: A Taliban official says official media have stopped showing images of living beings in some Afghan provinces to comply with morality laws.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Violence-hit Pakistan locks down the capital for an Asian security meeting
item.description: Pakistani authorities have locked down the capital in a major security move as senior officials from several nations arrive for an Asian security group meeting.
item.dc:creator: Munir Ahmed, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: How did a killing at a Sikh temple lead to Canada and India expelling each other's diplomats?
item.description: Relations between India and Canada are at a low point as the countries expelled each other’s top diplomats over an ongoing dispute about the killing of a Sikh activist in Canada.
item.dc:creator: Sheikh Saaliq, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: China unveils its plans to turn its feats in space exploration into scientific advances
item.description: BEIJING (AP) — China has made great strides in exploring space in recent years, rocketing astronauts to its own space station and bringing back rocks from the moon. Now it wants to turn those feats into scientific advances.
item.dc:creator: KEN MORITSUGU, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: A Hong Kong zoo seeks answers after 9 monkeys die in 2 days
item.description: Hong Kong’s oldest zoo is seeking answers in a monkey medical mystery after nine animals died in two days, including three members of a critically endangered species.
item.dc:creator: Kanis Leung, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: A pastry brought to Mexico by British miners is still popular after 200 years
item.description: REAL DEL MONTE, Mexico (AP) — Isabel Arriaga Lozano carefully fills a small pastry with a savory mix of meat, potatoes and chili pepper. She is crafting a “paste” (pronounced PAH-stay), a beloved Mexican snack with a rich history.
item.dc:creator: INDIA GRANT, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: China deploys record 125 warplanes in large-scale military drill in warning to Taiwan
item.description: TAIPEI, Taiwan (AP) — China employed a record 125 aircraft, as well as its Liaoning aircraft carrier and ships, in large-scale military exercises surrounding Taiwan and its outlying islands Monday, simulating the sealing off of key ports in a move that underscores the tense situation in the Taiwan S
item.dc:creator: JOHNSON LAI and HUIZHONG WU, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Campaigning begins for Japan's parliamentary election
item.description: TOKYO (AP) — Official campaigning for Japan's Oct. 27 parliamentary election began Tuesday with new Prime Minister Shigeru Ishiba seeking a mandate for his policies and for reforms after the governing party’s political funds scandal.
item.dc:creator: MARI YAMAGUCHI, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: North Korea blows up parts of inter-Korean road and rail links in a symbolic display of anger
item.description: South Korea says North Korea blew up the northern parts of inter-Korean road and rail links no longer in use.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Why US is sending troops, prized air defense system to Israel
item.description: The U.S. announced this week a major new air defense system for Israel, along with the deployment of 100 American troops to operate it in the country, in a development that will bolster Israeli security but potentially put American forces at risk.
item.dc:creator: Brad Dress, The Hill

item.title: U.S. to send troops and air defense system to Israel
item.description: The Pentagon announced the U.S. military will send a Terminal High Altitude Defense battery and about 100 U.S. troops to Israel.
item.dc:creator: Vinay Simlot, WGN 9

item.title: Pandas en route to National Zoo after a year
item.description: Two new giant pandas are reportedly en route to the National Zoo in Washington, D.C. “Something *giant* is coming to Washington, D.C. via the @FedEx Panda Express!” the zoo said in a post on social media platform X on Monday. “The Zoo will be closed to the public tomorrow, Oct. 15.
item.dc:creator: Tara Suter, The Hill

item.title: French citizen convicted in Russia of collecting military information and gets 3 years in prison
item.description: MOSCOW (AP) — A Russian court on Monday convicted a French citizen of collecting military information and sentenced him to three years in prison. Laurent Vinatier, who was arrested in Moscow in June, earlier admitted guilt, setting the stage for a fast-tracked trial.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Poland's leader defends his decision to suspend the right to asylum
item.description: WARSAW, Poland (AP) — Poland's Prime Minister Donald Tusk on Monday defended a plan to suspend the right to asylum as human rights and civil society organizations argued that fundamental rights must be respected.
item.dc:creator: VANESSA GERA, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: France’s far-right leader Marine Le Pen faces court on charges of embezzling EU funds
item.description: PARIS (AP) — French far-right leader Marine Le Pen strongly denied committing any wrongdoing at a Paris court Monday as she and her National Rally party stand trial over the suspected embezzlement of European Parliament funds.
item.dc:creator: SYLVIE CORBET, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: India-Canada diplomatic war intensifies over killing probe
item.description: India has strongly rejected Canada's allegations that its diplomats are "persons of interest" in a murder investigation, stating that the claims are part of Trudeau's "political agenda" and that Canada had failed to provide evidence to support its claims.
item.dc:creator: Sarakshi Rai, The Hill

item.title: North Koreans fighting in Ukraine alongside Russians, Zelensky says
item.description: Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelensky said Sunday that North Korean troops are now fighting alongside Russian forces in Ukraine, appearing to confirm reports that have been circulating in recent weeks.
item.dc:creator: Brad Dress, The Hill

item.title: Middle East latest: EU condemns attacks on peacekeepers in Lebanon
item.description: The European Union on Monday condemned attacks on U.N. peacekeepers in Lebanon and rejected Israeli allegations that the U.N. was keeping them there to obstruct military operations against Hezbollah.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: China surrounds Taiwan in military exercise against independence
item.description: China has launched massive military exercises surrounding Taiwan in response to the island refusing to follow the rule of the Communist Party.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: UN peacekeepers in southern Lebanon are in the crosshairs of Israel’s war on Hezbollah
item.description: BEIRUT (AP) — As the war between Israel and Lebanon's Hezbollah group escalates, a United Nations peacekeeping force in Lebanon is increasingly in the crosshairs, with Israeli troops firing at the peacekeepers' headquarters and positions several times in the past week.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Udine on high alert ahead of Italy's match against Israel
item.description: UDINE, Italy (AP) — The city of Udine is on high alert as Italy prepares to host Israel in a competitive soccer match on Monday.
item.dc:creator: DANIELLA MATAR, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Israel says 4 soldiers killed by Hezbollah drone attack while Israeli strike in Gaza leaves 20 dead
item.description: DEIR AL-BALAH, Gaza Strip (AP) — A Hezbollah drone attack on an army base in central Israel killed four soldiers and severely wounded seven others Sunday, the military said, in the deadliest strike by the militant group since Israel launched its ground invasion of Lebanon nearly two weeks ago.
item.dc:creator: WAFAA SHURAFA and SAMY MAGDY, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Imprisoned Belarus activist who was a symbol of defiance hasn't been heard from for months
item.description: The last time any of Maria Kolesnikova’s family had contact with the imprisoned Belarusian opposition activist was more than 18 months ago.
item.dc:creator: Yuras Karmanau, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Maybe advanced civilizations aren’t all that advanced: Scientist
item.description: Antonio Gelis-Filho writes that perhaps advanced civilizations have reached the limits of their technology, and we may be close to doing the same.
item.dc:creator: Rich Johnson, NewsNation

item.title: Interpol launches campaign to help solve 46 cold cases of women whose bodies were found in Europe
item.description: Interpol launched an initiative last week to seek the public's help in solving 46 cold cases of women whose bodies were discovered in Europe between 1982 and 2021.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Hezbollah drone attack in Israel kills multiple IDF soldiers, wounds dozens of people: reports
item.description: A drone strike in Binyamina, Israel, on Sunday killed multiple IDF soldiers and injured dozens of people, in an attack Hezbollah has claimed responsibility for.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Trump touts relationship with Kim Jong Un, Putin
item.description: Former President Trump on Sunday touted his relationships with North Korean leader Kim Jong Un and Russian President Vladimir Putin.
item.dc:creator: Lauren Irwin, The Hill

item.title: Former Scottish leader Alex Salmond, who nearly got Scotland's independence from UK, dies at 69
item.description: Alex Salmond, the former first minister of Scotland and a prominent advocate for Scottish independence, has died at the age of 69 in Ohrid, North Macedonia.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Biden: Israel should 'absolutely' stop strikes on UN peacekeepers in Lebanon
item.description: President Biden said Friday that Israel should “absolutely” stop strikes on the U.N. peacekeepers in Lebanon, following two incidents the group was involved in this week.
item.dc:creator: Filip Timotija, The Hill

item.title: Mount Everest remains believed to be climber who vanished 100 years ago
item.description: A National Geographic documentary crew has found the possible partial remains of Andrew Irvine, a British climber who vanished on Mount Everest 100 years ago.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: British YouTuber and rapper Yung Filly is charged with raping and choking a woman in Australia
item.description: PERTH, Australia (AP) — A British YouTuber and rapper known as Yung Filly has been charged with raping and choking a woman in a hotel room following an Australian music performance.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Israeli defense minister warns an attack on Iran would be 'lethal' and 'surprising'
item.description: JERUSALEM (AP) — Israel's defense minister warned on Wednesday that his country's retaliation for a recent Iranian missile attack will be “lethal” and “surprising,” while the Israeli military pushed ahead with a large-scale operation in northern Gaza and a ground offensive in Lebanon against Hezboll
item.dc:creator: TIA GOLDENBERG, SAMY MAGDY and WAFAA SHURAFA, Associated Press, NewsNation
